error.mixed_resources: Can't mix {a} and {b} in one sum.
error.need_resource: Tag the amount with a resource, e.g. “13400 ideo”.
error.no_data: That value isn't available right now.
error.parse: Can't read that — check the expression.
error.past_time: That time is in the past.

calc.help_hint: Amounts, incomes and durations combine naturally: try “until 13400 ideo”, “credits in 8h”, “afford 50k c” or “total = 9800 + lex slot”. Results update live as your income changes — and a pinned “until” or “afford” line pops a notification when it completes (even if that happened while you were away).

hint.afford: afford <cost> — can you pay it, and when. Add text at the end to label the reminder.
hint.at: <resource> at <time> — projection. Or: at <time> <your note> for a reminder note.
hint.in: <resource> in <duration> — projection. Or: in <duration> <your note> for a reminder note.
hint.until: until <amount> <res> — time until you have it. until +<amount> means “more than when you wrote it”.

calc.recent_empty: Lines you type in the calculator (X) land here.
calc.saved_empty: No pinned lines yet — pin a calculation to keep it updating here.
calc.title: Calculator

building.limited_hint: Can only build one per celestial body (planet, moon, asteroid).
building.unique_hint: Can only build one per star system.

sector.repartition: Number of star systems and dominions

character_reaction.attack_enemies: Interdiction, intercepts any unallied navarch arriving in its system.

topbar.bankrupt_tooltip: Your empire no longer has the financial means to support your agents. They have gone on strike and no longer obey orders. The Navarchs have also changed their reaction to 'Deserter'.

attacker.critical_failure: Brandishing a poorly balanced plasteel blade **{spy}** tracked down and attempted to eliminate **{target_player}**'s agent **{target}** . They failed and were likely detected by **{system}** security forces.
attacker.critical_success: Hidden under an RNA cloak and proceeding with shadow-like discretion, **{spy}** tracked down and eliminated **{target_player}**'s agent **{target}** on **{system}**.
attacker.normal_failure: Overwhelmed by the labyrinthian cityscapes, **{spy}** failed to track down and eliminate **{target_player}**'s agent **{target}** on **{system}**.
attacker.normal_success: Benefitting from their target's momentary carelessness, **{spy}** eliminated **{target_player}**'s agent **{target}** after having studied their movements on **{system}**.

defender.critical_failure: A bomb was placed in the heart of **{system}**'s citadelle. It exploded during an important conference killing **{target}** and many innocent bystanders.
defender.critical_success: **{target}**'s security detail thwarted an assassination attempt on **{system}**. The suspect managed to escape but their identity is known by authorities.
defender.normal_failure: While on **{system}** inspecting its largest O.P.U. , **{target}** was assassinated. Medics sent to the scene were unable to resuscitate the victim.
defender.normal_success: An assassination attempt took place on **{system}**. It was a close call, but **{target}** walked away with a minor flesh wound.

box.unknown_spy: An unknown enemy spy. Their identity remains a mystery.

colonization.description:
Congratulations! **{system}** is yours.

Under the watchful gaze of Navarch **{admiral}**, colonization efforts were successful.

It's now time to construct the system's first buildings.
colonization.title: Colonization of {system}

attacker.critical_failure: Navarch **{admiral}**'s troops were stopped in their tracks during their invasion of **{system}** by the strong defensive capabilities of **{system_player}**. Your fleet fell back on a nearby system.
attacker.critical_success:
Thanks to superior tactics, Navarch **{admiral}**'s troops completely decimated **{system_player}**'s defenses on **{system}**.



This system is now under your control.
attacker.normal_failure: After intense space and ground combat, **{system_player}**'s defenses on **{system}** held back Navarch **{admiral}**'s forces and caused them to retreat to a nearby system.
attacker.normal_success:
After difficult battles, Navarch **{admiral}**'s troops finally conquered **{system_player}**'s **{system}**.



This system is now under your control.

defender.critical_failure:
**{system}**'s defenses were utterly obliterated by Navarch **{admiral}**'s invasion forces acting under the orders of **{admiral_player}**.



You have lost control of this system.
defender.critical_success: The defenders of **{system}** merit high praises! They admirable pushed back Navarch **{admiral}**'s invasion forces acting under the orders of **{admiral_player}**.
defender.normal_failure:
Despite an admirable defense, Navarch **{admiral}**'s troops under the orders of **{admiral_player}** ended up completely invading **{system}**.



This system is now lost.
defender.normal_success: The defensive lines of **{system}** had their work cut out for them! But intense battles saw them victorious against Navarch **{admiral}**'s troops under the orders of **{admiral_player}**.

attacker.critical_success: You Siderian **{speaker}** successfully seduced **{target_player}**'s agent **{target}** on **{system}**.

description.critical_failure: Arriving unnoticed, **{spy}** did not manage to hack the internal surveillance network on **{player}**'s system **{system}**. They left traces that may lead back to them.

They installed a **{contact} nouveau malware**.

help.legend_uninhabited: Uninhabited or uninhabitable. No population, no owner.
help.legend_inhabited_neutral: Inhabited by a neutral NPC population. Not governed by any faction.
help.legend_inhabited_other: Inhabited and held by an opposing faction. Each opposing faction has its own overlay color.
help.legend_inhabited_self: One of your systems. The faint colored halo around it marks ownership — only your systems and dominions get it.
help.legend_navarch: One of your Navarchs in transit. You always see your own agents moving on the map, regardless of type.
help.legend_siderian: One of your Siderians in transit. Covert — invisible to enemy radar.
help.legend_erased: One of your Erased in transit. Covert — invisible to enemy radar.
help.legend_detected: A Navarch picked up by your radar (S.L.S.D.) — your own faction's other members and any opposing faction. Only the faction color and direction are revealed; the agent's identity is never disclosed.
help.legend_modes_title: Map view modes (toggles in the top-right)
help.legend_mode_visibility: Your intel level (1-5) on every visited system, plus a faint ring sized to that value. Unvisited systems show nothing — go scout something to see numbers appear.
help.legend_mode_population: The population point value of each system you have enough intel on (visibility 3+). The ring scales with the value. Systems below that intel threshold show nothing.
help.legend_mode_radar: Your detection range, drawn as a dashed boundary around your radar coverage. Enemy Navarchs that enter this disk appear as moving arrows. Siderians and Erased remain invisible.
help.legend_modes_hint: These overlays only render when zoomed in close to the map.
help.legend_note_asterisk: An asterisk (*) after a system name means the system is governed by a neutral NPC population or by an AI-managed dominion, not directly by a human player.
help.legend_note_detection: Detected arrows are always Navarchs. Siderians and Erased travel invisibly to all radar — you only learn they arrived after they act.
help.legend_note_allies: Faction-mates' Navarchs appear as anonymous detected blips when they enter your radar, the same way enemy Navarchs do. You only see your own agents in full detail.
help.stances_intro: Each Navarch carries a fleet stance that decides when the fleet picks fights on its own. Stances only ever apply to cross-faction admirals — faction-mates are never targeted. There are three moments at which a stance can trigger an engagement; the matrix below shows which ones each stance reacts to.
help.stances_flee_desc: Passive. The fleet tries to flee whenever it gets caught arriving at a system that already has hostile defenders. It never picks fights on its own.
help.stances_fight_back_desc: Passive. The fleet fights when directly attacked, but never initiates and never intervenes for an ally.
help.stances_defend_desc: Reacts to hostile actions inside its system — raids, looting, conquests, and colonization attempts. Defenders also join in if a faction-mate's fleet is directly attacked here. Two Defender fleets from opposing factions can sit in the same system without firing (armed neutrality).
help.stances_attack_enemies_desc: Watches the door. Intercepts any unallied Navarch arriving at its system, regardless of intent. Does NOT pick a fight when it is itself the arriving fleet — an Interdiction fleet can jump past a sitting enemy without engaging, which is what lets two cooperating factions both run Interdiction to lock down a system without firing on each other in transit.
help.stances_attack_everyone_desc: Always hot. Engages any unallied Navarch in the same system — when an enemy arrives, when an enemy starts a hostile action, AND when the Fury fleet itself arrives at a system that already has an unallied Navarch. The simplest setting if you want a fleet to always hit anything it sees.
help.stances_trigger_incoming: An unallied Navarch arrives at this fleet's system.
help.stances_trigger_hostile_action: An unallied Navarch starts a raid, looting, conquest, or colonization in this fleet's system.
help.stances_trigger_arriving: This fleet arrives at a system that already has an unallied Navarch.
help.stances_note_passive: Deserter and Prudent are purely reactive — they never appear in any of the engagement filters. A Prudent fleet still fights once attacked; a Deserter rolls to flee instead.
help.stances_note_idle: Only idle or docking defenders can intercept. A fleet already busy with a raid, jump, or fight cannot be pulled into another engagement.
help.stances_note_diplomacy: "Unallied" today means "not the same faction." If formal alliances are added later, allied factions will be excluded from these triggers the same way faction-mates already are.
help.faq_description: Player-maintained FAQ covering common questions about gameplay, mechanics, and strategy. Available in English and French.
help.wiki_description: Browse the community-maintained wiki for guides, lore, and detailed game mechanics. French only.

faction_government.disabled: This game does not have faction governments.
faction_government.founding_hint: Your faction is drafting its government's foundation. When the founding period ends, the first elections begin.

quorum_stages.0: The offering is dark — the faithful have pledged little.
quorum_stages.1: A faint glow — the offering grows, but wants.
quorum_stages.2: The candle gutters — the offering nears sufficiency.
quorum_stages.3: The flame burns — the offering suffices.

faction_government.title: Government
faction_government.distribute_hint: Distribute a share of the treasury evenly to all members
faction_government.challenge_hint: Stake credits to challenge the government (min 0.5% of faction wealth)
faction_government.withdraw_hint: Member withdrawals: up to {cap}% of each pool per 24h, taxed at market rates
faction_government.withdraw_disabled: Member withdrawals are disabled (the Head of Economy can set a cap)
faction_government.withdraw_cap_hint: Member withdrawal cap (% of each pool per member per 24h)
faction_government.grant_hint: Issue treasury to a member (free of cap and tax)
faction_government.overreach_hint: The Tetrarch may act in the Quaestor’s stead — each act costs the faction {malus}% of all income for 24 hours.
faction_government.tyranny_tooltip: The Tetrarch has been acting in the council’s stead. The malus fades as each act’s 24 hours run out.

operations.fight_fly: no longer respond to orders and flee.
operations.fight_leave: are no longer on the battlefield.
operations.fight_strike: for a total of **{damages} damages** ({hit_count} successful shots, {missed_count} missed shots, {crashed_count} destroyed units).

faction_diplomacy.title: Diplomacy
faction_diplomacy.privacy_hint: Standings are known only to the two factions involved. You cannot see how other factions stand with one another, and they cannot see yours.

cold_war.l1: The default standing: trade flows freely and no combat modifiers apply.
cold_war.l2: Hostile covert action (conquests, bombardments, removals) builds grievance between the two factions.
cold_war.l3: Grievance decays a little every game day; declaring war wipes the ledger clean.

war.l1: All market trade with this faction is embargoed.
war.l2: Their systems are harder for you to see (visibility −1), and yours for them.
war.l3: War sentiments are live: exhaustion climbs each day without conquests, momentum and frenzy move with every strike.
war.l4: The war ends only through a mutually accepted peace treaty.

non_aggression.l1: Mutual sensor openness: each side sees the other’s systems more clearly (visibility +1).
non_aggression.l2: Aggression under the pact is treason: it builds double grievance.
non_aggression.l3: Either leader may break the pact at any time, reverting to cold war.

faction_diplomacy.tension_ours_tooltip: Tension we hold against them, built by their conquests, bombardments and removals against us. It decays over time.
faction_diplomacy.tension_theirs_tooltip: Tension they hold against us, built by our conquests, bombardments and removals against them. It decays over time. Under a non-aggression pact, aggression counts double.

war_meters.exhaustion_tooltip: Builds each day the war drags on; taking enemy systems relieves it. A war without conquests wears the home front down.
war_meters.momentum_tooltip: Fed by destroying or sabotaging enemy fleets and by removing or seducing enemy Navarchs and Erased.
war_meters.frenzy_tooltip: The will to keep committing — and absorbing — atrocities. Spent by your own pillaging, destabilizing, bombardment and governor removals; replenished when the enemy does the same to you (double for bombardment and conquest).

faction_diplomacy.log_empty: Nothing on record with this faction yet.

production.unique_building: This building has already been constructed on this body.
production.unique_system: This building has already been constructed in this star system.

quotes.author: Arbuckle of Mogdora
quotes.content: When I saw Granis' palladium ornament at the Council of Tetrarchs, I got chills and I told myself: "That also is governing".
quotes.reference: Memoirs of a Dynast from Mogdora

quotes.author: Wylja Tantastr
quotes.content: How to annex the Bellegéroi, the Pulsians and the Quasastry in one hour? By offering freedom to all their inhabitants, whether biologic or not.

quotes.author: Rialna Firmagrand
quotes.content:
Zart picked me up and comforted me like no one had before.
So I decided to name my son after him.
To me, Zart was as good as any man, if not better.

quotes.author: Lulielle Gloriastre
quotes.content: I don't know a single android that deserves its Meditech card less than you do.

quotes.author: Ethiril of Belmezyr
quotes.content:
A promise to the new generations ?

Well... it's more freedom than the Tetrarchy has ever given so far; and I'm not only talking about the biological generations.

quotes.author: Endo of Arliport
quotes.content: Is this essential? If we don't ask ourselves this question, we will inevitably miss our target.

quotes.author: Alnadi Tahar
quotes.content: It is not a GEN-6 Omnifer, it is Soli Tionhan, owner of an H-Box on the 116th floor and my legitimate heir. He can leave at any time if he so wishes.

quotes.author: Lazr Ranmala
quotes.content: Whatever happens on Dor Alya or Lum Hen is not important as long as the traditions of each are respected.

quotes.author: Beryn Emerstone
quotes.content: For a while, I considered the wills of our androids to be a new market. But in the end we were running a deficit.

quotes.author: Cardanian proverb
quotes.content: Living is a gift, dying is a duty.

quotes.author: Ardeshir Kishon
quotes.content: The Others will return and only Cardan will be spared, because she tamed the machine without ever associating herself to it.

quotes.author: Peznoj Weldo
quotes.content: We screwed up everything on Bacha and in the end we didn't find a fraction of the ore we were looking for. The probes were defective.

quotes.author: Eleane of Dismagy
quotes.content: Myrmezir is a planet, a system, a foundation and today, even a tetrarchate.

quotes.author: Colborn Galtacon
quotes.content: They are nobody, nothing. They are not part of any census and only exist through their crimes.

quotes.author: Erlidge & Orman
quotes.content: According to legend, Siderian was a brilliant rhetorician of the past, today we call “siderian” a person who made speech his profession.
quotes.reference: Dictionary of proper names that became common

quotes.author: Corliss Demelza
quotes.content: You, a Navark! You don't possess one iota of the intelligence the Admirals of Akhena do!

quotes.author: Arledge Galtacon
quotes.content: They fear the coming of the Others who are mere superstition. However, they will not fear your destroyers, real as they may be.

quotes.author: Alba Valseciel
quotes.content: The Tetrarchs always thought that the Tetrarchy would last at least 4000 years. I believe that today they are overcome by doubt.

quotes.author: Baikin Shendo
quotes.content: Because of the A.R.K., peace has a heavier price than war in the Fringes.

quotes.author: Maiki Ongh
quotes.content: This planet had lush and beautiful vegetation. It was a sacred place to us.

quotes.author: Alba Valseciel
quotes.content:
Thanks to the lictors of the Tetrarchic High Court, I had to sentence an Erased.

I was one of the few judges to have had this opportunity.

quotes.author: Colleen Fridsky
quotes.content:
On Tetrane exists a palace...
Tetrane is a palace!

quotes.author: Azazine Uklahar
quotes.content:
Preserved from everything, Al-Agabra
Far from everything, Al-Agabra
Offers us everything, Al-Agabra
Most beautiful of all, Al-Agabra

quotes.author: Syn proverb
quotes.content: Far away from wars, life takes root.

resource-description.counter_intelligence: Intelligence renders more difficult the actions of Erased and agents present on this system.
resource-description.credit: Credits are used to construct buildings and ships, pay your agents' salaries, and maintain your fleets.
resource-description.defense: Defense protects this star system against pillagings, bombardments and conquests.
resource-description.habitation: Housing allows population to increase. Each housing building accommodates 1 billion people.
resource-description.happiness: Stability regulates your population's growth rate. Negative stability generates penalties on the star system.
resource-description.ideology: Ideology is used to purchase and apply lexes.
resource-description.mobility: Mobility represents how mobile your population is throughout the star system and increases the credits that your population earns you.
resource-description.production: Production increases the construction rate of your buildings and ships.
resource-description.radar: The S.L.S.D. increases the zone on the map in which neutral or enemy fleets are detected.
resource-description.remove_contact: Cybersecurity inspects and removes enemy malware placed on this star system (malware provides additional visibility)
resource-description.technology: Technology allows you to unlock patents and construct ships.
resource-description.workforce: Each billion inhabitants gives one population point. The population points make it possible to build new buildings, the population will then be mobilized.

empty_system.content: Nothing left to do but hide.
empty_system.label: Vacant system

hidden_system.content: Send an agent to scout to find out more.
hidden_system.label: No data available

status.inhabited_dominion: This system is a **dominion of {player}**. It develops itself independently and earns its owner resources. It can be **pillaged**, **bombarded** or **conquered** by a Navarch, and **subjugated** by a Siderian.
status.inhabited_neutral: This **autonomous system** develops itself independently and does not have an owner. It can be **pillaged**, **bombarded** or **conquered** by a Navarch, and **subjugated** by a Siderian.
status.inhabited_player: This system belongs to **{player}**. It can **pillaged**, **bombarded** or **conquered** by a Navarch.
status.own_inhabited_dominion: This system is your dominion. It develops itself independently and earns you resources
status.uninhabitable: This system is **uninhabitable**. It cannot be colonized.
status.uninhabited: This system is *uninhabited**. It can be **colonized** by a Navarch with a colonization ship.

tutorial.next_step_tooltip: Achieve the mission objectives to pass to the next step

step0.content:
Welcome to the Djarreh sector! Your faction has elevated you to the rank of dynast and believes in your abilities. There is no better academy than the Koshak Court for you to become one of the leading figures in the galaxy. The entire world sends its rising stars here to toughen them up and give them a sense of responsibility.

Myrmezir boasts of having liberated several systems from the grips of a conservative Syn clan. In truth, they have simply annexed a few strategic locations. Take back these strongholds in the name of the Synelectic Federation and prove your worth!
step0.title: Tutorial

step1.content: You can move around the map by simply clicking and dragging. You can also zoom in and out using your mouse's scroll wheel.
step1.title: Moving around on the map

step10.content: Well done! You are now producing ideology points. Ideology points, one of the game's three main resources, allow you to purchase and apply lexes and are required for recruiting certain agents.
step10.title: Ideology

step11.content: Open the lex panel and purchase the first available lex, Age of Exploration.
step11.title: Lex

step12.content: Lexes give powerful bonuses to your empire, but are sometimes accompanied by penalties.
step12.title: Lex

step13.content:
Unlike with patents, simply purchasing a lex does nothing. To take advantage of a lex's bonus, you will need to activate by applying it as a part of policy. A policy is a set of lexes activated at the same time that define the direction of your empire.

Activate the lex you just purchased. Click on the lex and then click the Policy Validation button.
step13.title: Policies

step14.content:
Agents are essential in your expansion. There are 3 types of agents: navarchs, erased and siderians. To deploy agents you need to unlock agent slots. These slots are unlocked through lexes like the one you just applied.

Now deploy a navarch by clicking on deploy agent.
step14.title: Agents

step15.content:
To exit the system view, use your scroll wheel and zoom out.

Your agent is now selected you can make them go to other systems by right clicking on them.

Send your agent exploring. This will allow you to scout out your surroundings and gain information on the systems around you.
step15.title: Exploration

step16.content: Well done! It's time to expand your system. Build 2 more Hive Cities.
step16.title: Development

step17.content: Be careful! Your stability is very low. Indeed, Hive Cities increase housing but lower the stability of your system. For a system to develop, you need vacant housing and positive stability. Some buildings and lexes bring stability penalties. In addition, each point of population in your system brings a point of negative stability.
step17.title: Stability

step18.content: Purchase the Urbanization patent that provides access to useful new buildings and build the Central Hub in the free tile indicated by the arrow. This infrastructure building provides stability.
step18.title: Stability

step19.content:
Star systems are composed of several celestial bodies that orbit the star at the center of your system. There are three types of celestial bodies: habitable planets, barren planets and orbital bodies (moons or asteroids). Each celestial body type allows for the construction of different buildings.

Habitable and barren planets require the construction of an infrastructure building before other buildings can be constructed there.
step19.title: Orbits and Celestial Bodies

step2.content:
This is your first system. It's your starting point and you will have to develop it to become stronger. If you lose it the game is over.

Click on the system to open it.
step2.title: Star system

step20.content:
Now build 2 Refining Ducts on the moon designated by the arrow. The Refining Ducts will increase the credit output of your system.

Each body has 3 potentials (industrial, scientific and appeal) that act as multipliers of the production bonuses brought forth by buildings. For example, building Refining Ducts on a body with high industrial potential will multiply their credit production, thus making them more efficient.
step20.title: Potentials

step21.content:
Now build 3 Arrays of Excavators on the open barren planet. These will increase the production of your system.

Production denotes a system's construction capabilities. The more production you have on a system, the faster buildings and ships will be constructed.
step21.title: Production

step22.content:
Great! Now we need to ramp up. Make sure you have an overall output of 200 credits, 50 technology points and 50 ideology points.

You'll need to buy new patents, build the right buildings in the right places and apply lexes to get there.
step22.title: Expand your empire

step23.content:
It's time to get your fleets ready and pillage nearby systems.

Pillaging and bombing are, along with conquering, navarch actions. Bombing will inflict heavy casualties among civilians and will damage a number of enemy buildings. Pillaging, on the other hand, will slightly damage the system but will allow you to gain many resources.
step23.title: 01 Pillaging

step24.content: First, bring your navarch back to your system. You'll need to arm them so that they can complete their mission.
step24.title: 01 Pillaging

step25.content: Now purchase the Assembly Lines patent. This will allow you to open a production line of small vessel.
step25.title: 01 Pillaging

step26.content: Construct the S-01 Assembly Line on a moon or asteroid with an open tile.
step26.title: 01 Pillaging

step27.content: Also purchase the Reinforced Fuselage patent. This patent will allow you to build fighter-bombers, very useful for pillaging or... bombing.
step27.title: 01 Pillaging

step28.content:
You can finally command 12 squadrons of 2 fighter-bombers.

First select your navarch (click on their name at the bottom right of the screen). You can assign a maximum of 18 squadrons to a navarch. These are organized in lines (l1 to l6) of 3 tiles. The first lines engage in combat before the subsequent ones.

Fill the first 4 lines with fighter-bombers. Your navarch will be stuck in the docking bay until construction is completed.
step28.title: 01 Pillaging

step29.content: You are now ready! This is your target. This system is an autonomous system, it develops on its own and is not player driven. Send your navarch there!
step29.title: 01 Pillaging

step3.content: Now construct your first building. Click on an empty tile and choose the Hive Cities building. It will take a few seconds to be constructed.
step3.title: Buildings

step30.content: Enter the system and click on the pillage action. Pillaging takes some time. You will be notified when it is completed. The success or failure of an action is always determined by the balance of power between the attacker and the defender. In the case of Pillaging, the attacker's bombing power is compared to the targeted system's defense.
step30.title: 01 Pillaging

step31.content: It's time to expand your empire, dynast! You must launch a colonization mission.
step31.title: 02 Colonization

step32.content: First, bring your navarch back to your system. They will serve as an escort for your colonization ship.
step32.title: 02 Colonization

step34.content: You can place a colonization ship on one of your navarch's empty tile's, preferably in the back lines. This ship does not require a shipyard in order to be constructed.
step34.title: 02 Colonization

step35.content: In the same way as for your agents, you will need to apply a lex to be allowed to administer an additional system. Purchase a policy slot and apply the Proto-Empire lex.
step35.title: 02 Colonization

step36.content: This is your target. This system is an empty system. It can be colonized but remains uninhabited for the moment.
step36.title: 02 Colonization

step37.content: Enter the system and click on the colonize action. Colonization takes some time and will consume your colonization ship. This action cannot fail.
step37.title: 02 Colonization

step38.content: Congratulations! Now you can manage your brand new system, build buildings and develop it.
step38.title: 02 Colonization

step39.content: Knowing your enemy is the key to victory. Your next mission will be to gather information about an important target. For that you will enlist the services of an <strong>erased agent<strong>.
step39.title: 03 Infiltration

step4.content:
Good! Your population is growing. Population will flow into your system as long as you have housing available.

Most buildings require a certain number of population points (labor) to be used. You can find all this information in the population panel.
step4.title: Population

step40.content: You can buy agents in the Agent Market, located at the top right of your screen. All players have access to the same market and when someone buys an agent, it will no longer be available to other players in the same game. Choose wisely and, most importantly, be quick!
step40.title: 03 Infiltration

step41.content: Now hire an erased one with at least one skill point in infiltration. If you can't find one, you can wait a while until one shows up in the market. New agents cycle in and out quite frequently.
step41.title: 03 Infiltration

step42.content: deploy your freshly recruited agent in your main system.
step42.title: 03 Infiltration

step43.content: This time we're going into enemy ranks! This Myrmezirian dominion will do just fine, send your erased agent there.
step43.title: 03 Infiltration

step44.content: You can now instruct your erased to infiltrate the network of the standalone system. Stealthy as a shadow, the erased agent will attempt to place malware, a covert wiretapping software.
step44.title: 03 Infiltration

step45.content: You've lost cover! An under cover erased agent is completely invisible to others. On the other hand, a detected erased agent is an easy target and will be unable to act. By waiting a while, the erased will slowly regain cover.
step45.title: 03 Infiltration

step46.content:
Malware allows you to increase the visibility you have on another system. At maximum visibility (out of 5 points), you will be able to see everything your enemy is planning on that system and they will b e non the wiser!

That said, depending on your targeted system's cybersecurity skill level, your malware could be discovered and removed.
step46.title: 03 Infiltration

step47.content: Repeatedly infiltrate this system's network now and increase visibility to 5!
step47.title: 03 Infiltration

step48.content:
You will now attempt to topple this system's government and take control of it.

You can conquer systems through military force but you can also make dominions. Dominions are systems that you do not directly control but that relinquish to you a part of their resource income. You will need to enlist the services of a siderian agent to have a dominions.
step48.title: 04 Taking Control

step49.content: To go ahead with this operation you will need to hire multiple siderian agents. You will therefore need to activate a new lex that will allow your empire to employ more than one siderian agent. Purchase, apply and activate the Public Relations lex.
step49.title: 04 Taking Control

step5.content: Now construct theDelta Polytec, the center for sciences. Click on an empty tile and select the proper building.
step5.title: Technologies

step50.content: Now find in the Agent Market a siderian agent with 2 to 3 destabilization skill points.
step50.title: 04 Taking Control

step51.content: Now find a second siderian agent with 2 to 3 propaganda skill points.
step51.title: 04 Taking Control

step52.content: We will intervene on this autonomous system. The visibility gained before will come in handy. Send your siderian agents there now.
step52.title: 04 Taking Control

step53.content: Use your siderian skilled in destabilization to launch a destabilization action on the system.
step53.title: 04 Taking Control

step54.content: This will temporarily lower the stability of the system. Stability is how a system defends itself against siderian actions so it is important to erode said stability in order to make it easier to take control.
step54.title: 04 Taking Control

step55.content: After any action, whether successful or unsuccessful, a siderian agents is going to have to take a rest.
step55.title: 04 Taking Control

step56.content: Just like with your systems, you will need to apply a lex to be allowed to own dominions. Buy a policy slot and apply the Exclusive Trade Zone lex.
step56.title: 04 Taking Control

step57.content: You can now, with your second siderian, take control of the autonomous system.
step57.title: 04 Taking Control

step58.content:
You've done it! You can now dive into your first team game.

Tetrarchy Falls is a highly cooperative game, so you'll have to find ways to work with your teammates in order to achieve the best result! Try a slower-paced, drawn out Legacy match to ease into the game or set fire to your keyboard by jumping straight into a quick-paced and frantic flash match.
step58.title: End of tutorial

step6.content: Well done! You are now producing technology points. Technology points, one of the three main resources in the game, allow you to buy patents and are needed to build certain ships and recruit certain agents.
step6.title: Technologies

step7.content: Open the patents panel and purchase the first available patent, the Holographic Storage System.
step7.title: Patents

step8.content: Patents unlock new buildings and ships. Each patent purchased increases the cost of all future patents, so choose wisely!
step8.title: Patents

step9.content: You can now build the Citadel, center for the arts and of thinkers! Click on an empty tile and select the proper building.
step9.title: Ideology
